query_text:
Sequential LLM decoding is slow because each token needs a full
large-model forward pass; how can a small draft model propose
tokens in parallel that the large model verifies without quality
loss?
paraphrase_essence: Sequential inference over N ordered decisions incurs O(N) expensive evaluations because each item must be processed in turn, creating a latency bottleneck even when many decisions could be made in parallel without degrading quality.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive decoding is slow because each token requires a full large-model forward pass over the growing context length; even with a KV cache, latency accumulates across decoding steps and attempts to parallelize risk increasing perplexity.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language generation, text decoding is slow because each token recomputes query-conditioned attention over dense visual tokens and image embeddings; cross-modal alignment is repeated at every step and reducing this cost can degrade VQA accuracy.
paraphrases.CV: In dense perception pipelines that iteratively decode a segmentation mask or sets of anchor boxes, inference is slow because each refinement step reprocesses the high-resolution feature map and attends over all patch tokens; latency scales with the number of regions despite most being background.
paraphrases.RL: In online control, action selection is slow because each decision requires serial rollouts to estimate the policy’s value function; wall-clock time grows with trajectory length, and naive concurrency harms sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, latency is dominated by cross-encoder reranking that scores every query–document pair in the candidate pool; relevance quality (e.g., recall@k) depends on this expensive query–document score even though many candidates are clearly non-relevant.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In streaming ASR, decoding is slow because each emitted token or phoneme requires attention over recent acoustic frames and repeated alignment updates; streaming latency accumulates across steps and parallelization attempts often increase WER.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In real-time manipulation, the sensorimotor loop is slow because each control cycle recomputes a motion plan using a high-fidelity dynamics model with contact reasoning; latency grows with planning horizon and maintaining manipulation policy stability limits opportunities for parallel computation.

seeds.lens:

large-model forward pass; how can a small draft model propose
tokens in parallel that the large model verifies without quality

relevance.reasoning: The seed's mechanism involves a small predictor (low-rank model) that anticipates which neurons will be active, enabling selective loading of weights — this is a form of cheap model predicting behavior of a larger model. The query requires a small draft model proposing tokens that a large model verifies (speculative decoding). While both involve a small model guiding computation of a larger model, the seed's mechanism is about weight loading/sparsity prediction for flash memory constraints, not token proposal and parallel verification. The underlying "small predictor guides large model computation" idea has thematic overlap, but the specific mechanism (neuron activation prediction for flash I/O reduction) does not transfer to the token drafting/verification pipeline without substantial redesign.

relevance.reasoning: The seed describes Picard iteration for parallelizing sequential trajectory simulation by splitting time-steps across processes and using cached values to enable batched policy evaluation. The query asks about speculative decoding where a small draft model proposes tokens in parallel that a large model verifies. Both involve parallelizing an inherently sequential process using cached/approximate intermediate values, but the seed's mechanism (Picard iteration with convergence guarantees for MDP trajectories) is quite different from the proposer/verifier paradigm of speculative decoding. The seed has no draft-model/verifier structure, no token-level speculation, and no acceptance/rejection mechanism — the core machinery of speculative decoding. The thematic overlap (parallelizing sequential computation) exists but the mechanisms don't map actionably.

relevance_lens.reasoning: (a) The seed's mechanism is Picard iteration applied to policy simulation: split a sequential trajectory across parallel processes, each evaluating the policy on its assigned timesteps using cached actions from others, then iteratively converging to the true sequential trajectory. (b) The lens reframes the query as a serial rollout bottleneck in online control, bridging to parallel trajectory simulation methods. (c) The Picard iteration mechanism — where parallel workers propose values using cached/draft information and iteratively refine toward the sequential solution — is structurally analogous to speculative decoding, where a draft model proposes tokens in parallel and a verifier corrects them. However, the seed's mechanism is about simulating a fixed policy trajectory (not a generative model), and convergence is guaranteed by the MDP structure rather than by a verification step that accepts/rejects proposals. The analogy is real but requires non-trivial adaptation: the "cache of actions" maps loosely to draft tokens, but the verification/rejection mechanism central to speculative decoding has no direct counterpart in Picard iteration for MDPs.

query_text:
VLM inference latency scales with visual token count but most
tokens are redundant; how can we identify and remove or merge
low-information visual tokens at inference without retraining
the encoder?
paraphrase_essence: Inference cost grows with the number of input units because all units are processed even when many are redundant, and with a fixed upstream representation there is no way to drop or aggregate low-information units without risking quality.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive inference is slow when context length is large because each decoding step attends over all prompt tokens in the KV cache; many tokens contribute little to the next-token distribution, yet their states must be retained, increasing compute and memory without meaningful perplexity gains.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language models, latency scales with visual token count because query-conditioned attention traverses every image embedding at each step; background patches add little to cross-modal alignment, yet all tokens are processed, raising inference time without improving VQA accuracy.
paraphrases.CV: For dense vision, processing is costly because the network operates over the entire feature map as patch tokens at high spatial resolution; large background regions are low-saliency, yet the fixed receptive field forces scoring everywhere, inflating latency for producing a segmentation mask.
paraphrases.RL: In online control, per-step compute grows with observation dimensionality because the policy and value function consume the full state each rollout; many inputs are weakly coupled to the reward signal, yet they cannot be omitted, slowing interaction and reducing sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval pipelines, reranking is slow when the candidate pool is large because a cross-encoder must compute a query-document score for every item; many candidates are redundant, yet with a fixed embedding index they cannot be suppressed before expensive scoring, increasing latency without gains in recall@k.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In streaming ASR, decoding is slow when the acoustic frame sequence is long because the model attends across a wide spectrogram and alignment window; extended silence or steady phonemes add little information, yet all frames are processed, increasing streaming latency without lowering WER.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In real-time manipulation, the sensorimotor loop slows when observations are dense because the manipulation policy and dynamics model must process all sensed features; much of this data is irrelevant to contact reasoning, yet motion plans are computed over the full state, risking control delays without task benefit.

query_text:
Inputs to generative models vary in complexity but standard
decoding allocates fixed compute per token; how can a model
adaptively allocate more or fewer computation steps without
degrading quality?
paraphrase_essence: Uniform per-decision compute budgets ignore instance difficulty, causing wasted computation on easy cases and degraded quality on hard cases under tight latency or resource constraints.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive decoding allocates a fixed FLOP budget per token and decoding step regardless of uncertainty; this static compute wastes KV cache lookups on easy continuations and increases perplexity at difficult positions when latency is constrained.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Multimodal captioning and VQA pipelines run a constant amount of query-conditioned attention over visual tokens independent of scene complexity; this uniform compute over-processes well-aligned regions and depresses VQA accuracy on poorly aligned content when inference budgets are tight.
paraphrases.CV: Vision backbones apply fixed-depth computation to every patch token at full spatial resolution regardless of saliency; this uniform processing expends feature-map updates on background and degrades segmentation mask fidelity on fine structures when compute is capped.
paraphrases.RL: Online control commonly uses a fixed number of rollouts and value-function backups per policy step irrespective of state uncertainty; this constant planning budget inflates decision latency in easy parts of a trajectory and reduces cumulative reward in hard regimes when the rollout budget is bounded.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Retrieval systems assign a fixed reranking budget to the candidate pool, invoking a cross-encoder to compute query-document scores uniformly; this static allocation spends compute on obvious negatives and lowers recall@k on ambiguous queries under strict latency constraints.
paraphrases.SPEECH: ASR decoders expend a fixed amount of computation per acoustic frame and hypothesized phoneme regardless of local ambiguity in the spectrogram; this uniform budget increases streaming latency on easy segments and raises WER at challenging phoneme alignments when compute is constrained.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In real-time control, the sensorimotor loop allocates a fixed planning or optimization budget per cycle for the manipulation policy independent of task difficulty; this constant-time allocation wastes dynamics-model evaluations during free-space motion and harms contact reasoning in cluttered interactions when the motion-plan iteration cap is tight.

query_text:
Long-context LLMs accumulate large KV caches that dominate
memory bandwidth; how can we compress or selectively evict
cache entries while preserving the model's ability to attend
to distant relevant tokens?
paraphrase_essence: In long-horizon sequence processing, the working memory grows with context length and its storage/transfer dominates runtime resources; aggressively reducing this state degrades the model’s ability to exploit rare long-range dependencies.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: During long-context decoding, the KV cache grows linearly with the number of tokens and saturates memory bandwidth across attention heads; truncating past cache entries effectively shortens context length and increases perplexity by preventing attention to distant tokens.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language models over long image/video sessions, the cache of visual tokens and cross-modal attention states becomes the dominant memory traffic; dropping image embeddings or earlier captions disrupts cross-modal alignment and reduces VQA accuracy on references to far-back frames.
paraphrases.CV: For video transformers and long-range perception, maintaining attention over many patch tokens across a wide temporal window makes attention activations dominate bandwidth; naive truncation of the feature map history shrinks the receptive field and breaks long-term object association in segmentation masks.
paraphrases.RL: In partially observed control with long rollout horizons, policies that condition on full trajectory history incur high memory traffic to retain past features; limiting the accessible history impairs credit assignment from delayed reward signals and degrades performance on temporally extended tasks.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In session-aware retrieval, retaining long interaction context and per-candidate features causes query–document scoring and cache accesses to dominate I/O; trimming the session history reduces reranking fidelity and lowers recall@k by obscuring distant but relevant signals in the candidate pool and embedding index.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In streaming attention-based ASR on long utterances, the decoder’s context over acoustic frames grows with time and saturates memory bandwidth; pruning the attention context disrupts phoneme alignment and increases WER, especially when distant coarticulation cues must be referenced under tight streaming latency.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In long-horizon manipulation, attention-based manipulation policies that maintain an attention memory over past observations and actions across the sensorimotor loop become bandwidth-bound on onboard hardware; shortening the history window degrades contact reasoning and undermines temporally extended motion plans.

query_text:
Low-bit quantization preserves perplexity but degrades emergent
abilities like in-context learning and chain-of-thought; how
can we quantize weights and activations while protecting the
components most responsible for these abilities?
paraphrase_essence: Uniform low-bit quantization calibrated to preserve aggregate loss corrupts a small set of sensitive internal computations that enable higher-order generalization, so complex behaviors degrade while surface metrics remain stable.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Weight and activation quantization that matches perplexity corrupts a subset of attention heads and KV cache dynamics over long context length, so in-context examples and chain-of-thought behaviors fail despite unchanged next-token loss.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Low-bit quantization that leaves caption quality intact distorts cross-modal alignment in query-conditioned attention over visual tokens, so VQA accuracy and compositional grounding collapse under multi-hop queries.
paraphrases.CV: Activation/weight quantization that maintains classification accuracy injects noise into high spatial-resolution feature maps and disrupts effective receptive fields, so small-object localization and segmentation masks degrade on detailed scenes.
paraphrases.RL: Parameter quantization that preserves average reward signal per rollout perturbs the policy’s sensitive components that govern exploration and long-horizon control, so trajectories become brittle even though short-horizon returns appear unchanged.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Low-bit compression of the embedding index that keeps average query-document scores stable disproportionately corrupts signals needed to separate hard negatives, so recall@k drops and downstream reranking with a cross-encoder suffers.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Quantization tuned to hold WER constant on short clips perturbs spectrogram and acoustic frame representations that maintain long-range phoneme alignment, so long-form decoding and prosodic consistency degrade even as headline error rates remain similar.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Low-precision networks that preserve nominal tracking corrupt computations essential for contact reasoning within the sensorimotor loop of a manipulation policy, so execution fails under discontinuous dynamics and tight motion plans despite similar performance in free-space tasks.

query_text:
Updating billions of parameters to adapt a pretrained LLM is
prohibitive on consumer hardware; how can we adapt the model
with a small number of additional parameters and match full
fine-tuning performance?
paraphrase_essence: Full-parameter fine-tuning of large pretrained models is compute/memory prohibitive on resource-limited hardware, while restricting adaptation to a small trainable parameter budget typically sacrifices target-task performance.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Updating all transformer layers of a pretrained LLM with billions of weights is memory- and compute-heavy on consumer GPUs—long context length inflates per-token activations across attention heads—while constraining adaptation to a small number of trainable parameters typically raises perplexity and weakens downstream generalization.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: End-to-end fine-tuning of a vision–language model across the image encoder and text decoder is prohibitive on limited hardware; limiting trainable parameters disrupts cross-modal alignment over visual tokens and image embeddings, reducing VQA accuracy under query-conditioned attention.
paraphrases.CV: Full fine-tuning of a high-capacity backbone and detection/segmentation heads is costly because high spatial resolution inputs produce large feature maps and wide receptive fields; restricting updates to a small parameter budget degrades segmentation mask quality and small-object accuracy.
paraphrases.RL: Adapting a pretrained agent by updating all parameters of the policy and value function is sample-inefficient and compute-intensive per rollout; forcing adaptation into a tiny trainable subset often fails under shifted reward signals and degrades return over long trajectories.
paraphrases.IR_REC: End-to-end retraining of a large cross-encoder or dual-encoder to handle domain shift is prohibitive on commodity GPUs and triggers costly rebuilding of the embedding index; constraining updates to few parameters degrades query-document scoring and lowers recall@k during reranking.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Updating all parameters of a large ASR model is memory-bound because long spectrograms yield many acoustic frames per utterance, while forcing adaptation into a small parameter set perturbs phoneme alignment and increases WER under streaming latency constraints.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Re-optimizing all weights of a high-capacity manipulation policy on real hardware is infeasible within the sensorimotor loop; constraining adaptation to a small trainable subset fails to capture contact reasoning and shifts in the dynamics model, degrading sim-to-real task success.

relevance.reasoning: The query asks for parameter-efficient adaptation of LLMs using a small number of additional parameters to match full fine-tuning. The seed describes a hypernetwork-based approach that generates adapter/prefix weights from a shared hypernetwork, freezing the PLM and training only a tiny fraction of parameters. This is directly a parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) mechanism. However, the seed focuses on multi-task efficiency via a shared hypernetwork (reducing per-task parameters), while the query is about single-model adaptation on consumer hardware. The core mechanism—freezing PLM weights and training only small additional parameters (adapters/prefixes)—is directly relevant to the query's problem, though the hypernetwork sharing aspect is an extra design element not strictly required by the query.

relevance.reasoning: The query asks about parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) — adapting LLMs with a small number of additional parameters (e.g., LoRA, adapters) to match full fine-tuning on consumer hardware. The seed's mechanism is about differentially private zeroth-order optimization (DPZero) that avoids backpropagation by using forward-pass finite differences, primarily motivated by privacy constraints. While the seed does address memory efficiency on commodity GPUs, its mechanism (scalar finite-difference gradient estimation + DP noise injection) is not about adding a small number of trainable parameters — it still updates all model parameters, just without backprop. The query's core requirement (small additional parameters, not full parameter updates) is not addressed by this seed's mechanism.

query_text:
Vision-language models trained on web data degrade when images
come from a shifted distribution like medical or satellite;
how can we maintain cross-modal alignment under unseen domain
shift without target-domain labels?
paraphrase_essence: Alignment learned on a source distribution breaks under unseen domain shift, causing performance to degrade when target-domain supervision is unavailable to recalibrate representations.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: LLMs pretrained on web text fail on out-of-domain prompts when no in-context examples are available; attention heads misinterpret domain-specific tokens and perplexity spikes under fixed context length.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Vision–language encoders trained with web captions lose cross-modal alignment on medical or satellite imagery, as image embeddings and visual tokens drift and query-conditioned attention attends off-target regions, degrading VQA accuracy in the absence of target-domain captions.
paraphrases.CV: Image backbones trained on natural photos produce feature maps that are miscalibrated on high–spatial resolution medical or remote-sensing inputs, where receptive fields capture spurious texture and segmentation masks or anchor boxes degrade without target-domain annotations.
paraphrases.RL: A policy trained from source-domain trajectories fails under dynamics shift when target-domain rollouts and reward signals are unavailable, yielding poor sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Encoders trained on general corpora yield miscalibrated query-document scores in a domain-specific collection, so recall@k drops because the embedding index no longer preserves neighborhoods and cross-encoder reranking cannot be trained without relevance labels.
paraphrases.SPEECH: An ASR model trained on web speech shows elevated WER on unseen domains, as phoneme alignment in the spectrogram drifts and streaming latency constraints limit context while target-domain transcripts are unavailable.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: A manipulation policy tuned in simulation fails in real hardware under sim-to-real visual and contact shifts, as the sensorimotor loop and dynamics model are miscalibrated and no task-level reward signal is available.

query_text:
Object detectors fail on rare classes with few or no labeled
instances; how can we detect and localize long-tail or novel
objects without per-class supervision?
paraphrase_essence: Category-specific supervision leads to brittle generalization: performance collapses on long-tail or novel instances when labeled examples are scarce or absent.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive language models exhibit elevated perplexity and miscalibrated token probabilities for tail vocabulary and novel entities when no in-context examples are available, causing errors to compound across decoding steps.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Vision–language models show weak cross-modal alignment for rare object categories, where image embeddings lack discriminative visual tokens and query-conditioned attention fails to localize them, yielding degraded VQA accuracy and caption grounding on novel categories.
paraphrases.CV: Dense detectors that learn per-class logits under heavy class imbalance fail to localize rare categories: feature maps devote low saliency to tail instances and anchor boxes poorly match atypical aspect ratios, degrading bounding box quality at inference.
paraphrases.RL: In goal-conditioned reinforcement learning, a policy trained from sparse reward signals fails on long-tail goals absent from the trajectory data, as the value function is unreliable off-distribution and rollouts rarely explore those regions of state space.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval and recommendation, cold-start and tail items with few interactions are missing or underweighted in the candidate pool, the embedding index yields weak representations, and query-document scores remain low, causing recall@k to degrade before reranking.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Automatic speech recognition exhibits spikes in WER for rare words and novel named entities without labeled pronunciations, as acoustic frame–to–phoneme alignment becomes unstable and spectrogram cues are insufficient for robust decoding.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In manipulation, a sensorimotor loop trained on frequent object geometries fails to generalize contact reasoning to novel objects, so the manipulation policy and dynamics model produce motion plans that break under previously unseen shape and material properties.

query_text:
Online RL is expensive but offline data suffers from
distribution mismatch between behavior and target policy;
how can we learn a strong policy from logs alone while
avoiding extrapolation errors?
paraphrase_essence: Learning a high-performing policy solely from logged data is unreliable because off-policy distribution shift forces extrapolation beyond the dataset support, while collecting on-policy interactions is costly.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Learning a response policy only from logged prompts–completions collected under a different decoding policy suffers distribution shift across tokens: at inference each decoding step visits contexts absent from the logs, inflating perplexity, while gathering on-policy samples is costly due to large context length and KV cache updates.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: With only offline captions and VQA traces from a fixed annotator policy, query-conditioned attention drifts because the image embedding–text distribution at test time differs, so cross-modal alignment outside the logged support degrades VQA accuracy while collecting interactive visual tokens from users is costly.
paraphrases.CV: Training dense predictors solely on offline frames from a narrow domain fails under deployment shift because feature map statistics and anchor box priors learned at one spatial resolution do not cover new viewpoints, degrading segmentation masks while acquiring on-policy video with dense annotations is costly.
paraphrases.RL: When only logged trajectories from a behavior policy are available, the target policy queries state–action regions with little support, causing value function extrapolation and overestimation, while obtaining corrective rollouts with sufficient exploration is costly due to poor sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Learning a new ranker only from click logs collected under a prior system induces selection bias: query-document scores for unseen parts of the candidate pool are extrapolated during reranking, harming recall@k, while unbiased online traffic for a cross-encoder is costly to obtain.
paraphrases.SPEECH: ASR trained from offline corpora with teacher-forced alignment suffers exposure bias at test time because during streaming decoding the acoustic frame and phoneme context distribution diverges from training, inflating WER, while collecting on-policy user speech under strict streaming latency is costly.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Learning a manipulation policy solely from teleoperation logs breaks under deployment because the sensorimotor loop visits contact states outside the logged support, forcing the dynamics model to extrapolate during a motion plan, while on-robot rollouts are costly due to sim-to-real and safety constraints.

paraphrases.ROBOTICS: A manipulation policy optimized in simulation with an imperfect dynamics model and idealized sensing breaks in the real sensorimotor loop due to unmodeled contact reasoning and actuation effects, while sim-to-real adaptation is constrained by very few demonstrations.

query_text:
Complex sequential tasks have nested goal structure but flat
policies struggle with credit assignment over long horizons;
how can we learn or impose a hierarchy that decomposes the
task and accelerates learning?
paraphrase_essence: When tasks span long horizons with nested subgoals, flat learners face sparse and delayed credit assignment, leading to inefficient exploration and slow, unstable learning.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: In long-form generation, optimizing token likelihood over large context length is slow to improve global structure because each decoding step receives delayed and diffuse credit; perplexity underweights discourse-level objectives so early tokens get little useful feedback.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In multi-hop visual question answering, end-to-end scoring over visual tokens with query-conditioned attention fails to attribute credit to intermediate reasoning steps; cross-modal alignment errors accumulate over long chains and VQA accuracy degrades with reasoning depth.
paraphrases.CV: In long video understanding, per-frame models with limited receptive field and fixed anchor boxes receive supervision primarily from a final action label or segmentation mask, so early feature maps get delayed credit and accuracy drops as clip length increases.
paraphrases.RL: In sparse-reward, long-horizon control, a single policy and value function fail to propagate credit across extended trajectories; reliable return estimates require many rollouts and exploration is inefficient, yielding poor sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In session-based retrieval, treating each turn with a flat query-document score and one-shot reranking fails to capture evolving subgoals across the session; the candidate pool is repeatedly expanded without effective attribution, so recall@k improves slowly.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In long utterances, mapping spectrograms to text at the acoustic frame level provides delayed supervision because early phoneme alignment errors are only penalized at the sequence end; WER rises with duration and streaming latency constraints exacerbate the issue.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In long-horizon assembly, a monolithic manipulation policy in a tight sensorimotor loop receives sparse task-level feedback; mistakes in early contact reasoning are only exposed after full execution, and without intermediate motion plan attribution data demands on the dynamics model become prohibitive.

query_text:
Generating intermediate reasoning steps before the final
answer sharply improves LLM accuracy; how can we apply
analogous intermediate-computation patterns to vision,
reinforcement learning, or recommendation models?
paraphrase_essence: Systems forced to produce a final decision in a single pass underperform on tasks that implicitly require multi-step computation, because intermediate latent structure is neither represented nor supervised.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Answer accuracy fails on compositional prompts when next-token decoders compress multi-step reasoning into a single decoding step; token-level perplexity can remain low while the absence of intermediate tokens in in-context examples leaves no aligned latent structure.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Cross-modal generation degrades on compositional VQA when image embeddings are mapped to captions in one shot; query-conditioned attention lacks intermediate visual tokens for referent grounding, weakening cross-modal alignment and reducing VQA accuracy.
paraphrases.CV: Dense vision models fail on complex layouts when outputs are decoded directly from a single feature map of patch tokens; limited receptive field at high spatial resolution yields inconsistent segmentation masks across long-range dependencies and occlusions.
paraphrases.RL: Control performance collapses on sparse-reward tasks when a policy must solve long rollout horizons with only terminal feedback; value function estimation and trajectory credit assignment are ill-conditioned without intermediate structure along the trajectory.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Retrieval quality drops for multi-facet queries when a single query-document score is computed per item and a large candidate pool is reranked without intermediate constraint aggregation; recall@k suffers even with a high-coverage embedding index.
paraphrases.SPEECH: ASR accuracy degrades on long-form speech when a decoder emits transcripts directly from the spectrogram without intermediate alignment; acoustic frame-to-phoneme uncertainty remains unresolved, inflating WER.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Task success rates fall in long-horizon manipulation when a manipulation policy operates in a reactive sensorimotor loop without an intermediate motion plan; contact reasoning under imperfect dynamics models becomes unstable and error-prone.

query_text:
Models attending to long contexts (videos, long documents,
episodes) suffer from quadratic attention cost and forgetting;
how can we maintain effective memory of distant information
without exhausting compute?
paraphrase_essence: Maintaining accurate access to long-range context becomes unreliable and compute-prohibitive as sequence length grows, because attention and memory scale superlinearly while models forget distant information under fixed budgets.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Long-context language modeling is costly because per-token decoding scales with context length due to quadratic attention and a growing KV cache; under fixed attention-head capacity the model forgets early tokens, increasing perplexity on distant references.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Video-language models are compute-bound when query-conditioned attention over visual tokens grows quadratically with clip length; cross-modal alignment to early image embeddings weakens, reducing VQA accuracy on long-range temporal queries.
paraphrases.CV: In video segmentation and tracking, global attention over patch tokens at high spatial resolution is expensive because cost grows quadratically with frame count and area; limited receptive fields cause drift and failure to maintain correspondences, degrading segmentation masks over long clips.
paraphrases.RL: Long-horizon RL is inefficient because maintaining trajectory context across extended rollouts requires large memory and long unrolls, and the policy forgets early observations, impeding credit assignment from delayed reward signals and hurting sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Long-document reranking is costly because cross-encoder query-document scoring scales with token count, and relevant spans far from the query are underweighted, lowering recall@k within a fixed candidate pool from the embedding index.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Long-form ASR is slow and unstable when attention over acoustic frames from the spectrogram scales quadratically; alignment drifts lead to forgetting of distant context, increasing WER and streaming latency.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In long-horizon manipulation, conditioning the manipulation policy on full history in the sensorimotor loop is costly because internal state and dynamics model unrolls grow with episode length; limited capacity causes loss of long-range contact reasoning and degraded motion plan execution.

query_text:
Dense models compute every parameter for every input, wasting
compute when only a fraction is needed; how can we conditionally
activate only the relevant subnetwork at inference?
paraphrase_essence: Uniform dense inference expends full model compute on every input instance, causing latency and energy to scale with total parameter count rather than input-specific relevance when only a small subset is needed.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Autoregressive LLM inference is compute-inefficient because each decoding step evaluates all layers across every token using all attention heads, with per-step FLOPs unchanged by token difficulty; the KV cache reduces memory but not this layerwise cost, so latency scales with context length rather than token-level relevance.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Vision–language models are slow at inference because query-conditioned attention performs dense cross-modal alignment over all visual tokens and text tokens, even when only a few image embeddings are pertinent to the query, inflating compute for a given VQA accuracy target.
paraphrases.CV: Dense vision backbones are costly because every patch token is propagated through full-depth feature maps at the input spatial resolution, despite most regions being background; inference time scales with image size rather than the support of the segmentation mask or active anchor boxes.
paraphrases.RL: In online control, the policy and value function incur a full forward pass at every rollout step, so per-step compute remains high even when trajectories contain sparse task-relevant state features, degrading real-time responsiveness and sample efficiency for long-horizon tasks under a fixed reward signal budget.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Retrieval pipelines are compute-bound when reranking requires a cross-encoder to score the entire candidate pool per query, forcing dense query–document interactions even though the query-document score depends on a small subset, so latency grows with pool size and constrains recall@k.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Streaming ASR wastes compute by processing each acoustic frame with the full network regardless of phoneme boundaries or silence, applying dense operations over the spectrogram that do not adapt to alignment difficulty, which elevates streaming latency without commensurate gains.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Closed-loop manipulation is compute-heavy because the sensorimotor loop runs the full manipulation policy and dynamics model each cycle across the entire motion plan, even when contact reasoning is only required for a small subset of interactions, leading to unnecessary latency under real-time constraints.

query_text:
Large generalist models are accurate but expensive; smaller
specialists are cheap but worse on the specialist's own
domain; how can we transfer the relevant generalist capability
to a small specialist?
paraphrase_essence: Critical domain-specific competence resides in a high-accuracy but expensive generalist and is unavailable to a lightweight specialist, yielding inferior in-domain performance at acceptable cost.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Small domain-specialist language models suffer higher perplexity and task error because broad priors encoded across attention heads are unavailable to them, while using a large generalist at each decoding step is costly in KV-cache memory and compute at typical context lengths.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language reasoning, compact domain-specific VQA models underperform because robust cross-modal alignment and query-conditioned attention learned by a generalist are unavailable, yet invoking the generalist with many visual tokens per input inflates per-query latency and compute, degrading VQA accuracy–efficiency trade-offs.
paraphrases.CV: For dense perception, small backbones produce weaker segmentation masks in-domain because semantically rich feature maps with large receptive fields from a generalist encoder are unavailable, while evaluating all patch tokens at high spatial resolution with the large backbone is computationally prohibitive.
paraphrases.RL: In reinforcement learning, a lightweight task-specific policy attains low return and poor sample efficiency because transferable structure in the generalist policy and value function over trajectories is unavailable, whereas training or querying the generalist over long rollout horizons is compute- and time-intensive.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, a small domain-specific dual-encoder yields lower recall@k because fine-grained query–document signals captured by a generalist cross-encoder are unavailable, but reranking a large candidate pool with that cross-encoder requires expensive per-pair scoring and incurs substantial latency.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In ASR, compact domain-specialist models exhibit higher WER because robust phoneme–grapheme alignment and language priors from a generalist are unavailable, while running the generalist over long spectrograms at the acoustic-frame rate increases streaming latency and compute.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In manipulation, a lightweight task-specific manipulation policy is brittle because contact reasoning and dynamics model priors present in a generalist are unavailable, yet executing the generalist within a high-frequency sensorimotor loop to generate motion plans imposes prohibitive compute and latency for real-time control.

query_text:
Test-time distributions diverge from training data along axes
the model never saw; how can we make models robust to such
shifts without observing the target distribution at training?
paraphrase_essence: Models trained on one distribution fail under test-time shifts along unseen factors because the target distribution is unavailable during training.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Language models trained on a source token distribution show elevated perplexity and brittle decoding steps when prompts shift in domain, syntax, or context length beyond training support, with no target-domain in-context examples available during training.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision-language models, cross-modal alignment degrades when test inputs contain visual tokens and linguistic cues outside the training support, causing query-conditioned attention to mislocalize and VQA accuracy to drop, with no target-domain image embeddings seen during training.
paraphrases.CV: Vision systems trained on a source distribution fail when test-time scenes shift in texture, viewpoint, or scale, producing feature map statistics that mismatch learned receptive fields, miscalibrated anchor boxes, and degraded segmentation masks across spatial resolutions while target data are unavailable during training.
paraphrases.RL: A policy optimized under a nominal MDP suffers performance collapse when deployment induces unseen dynamics or reward signal shifts, as rollouts visit trajectory distributions never encountered during training and the value function is miscalibrated, with no target-domain experience available beforehand.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, an embedding index trained on source queries/documents yields miscalibrated query-document scores when test-time intents and document styles shift, causing recall@k to drop and cross-encoder reranking to operate suboptimally, with no target-domain signals available during training.
paraphrases.SPEECH: ASR models trained on a source corpus exhibit elevated WER when spectrograms shift due to unseen accents, microphones, or noise, disrupting acoustic frame–phoneme alignment, with no target-domain audio available during training.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Robotic systems tuned in simulation fail at deployment when the sensorimotor loop encounters unseen friction, compliance, or lighting, because the manipulation policy and dynamics model lack exposure for contact reasoning in those regimes (sim-to-real shift), with no target-environment data available during training.

query_text:
Spurious correlations make models rely on shortcuts that fail
under intervention; how can we generate counterfactual examples
that break these shortcuts and induce invariance to non-causal
features?
paraphrase_essence: Models trained on observational data latch onto spurious correlations, so performance collapses under interventions that decouple non-causal features from the target because counterfactual coverage over those factors is missing.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: In language modeling, attention heads over-attend to shortcut tokens learned from observational corpora, so perplexity spikes and predictions flip under prompt interventions or paraphrase changes; counterfactual in-context examples that vary surface tokens while preserving semantics are largely unavailable within the context length.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language pretraining, cross-modal alignment often binds captions to background visual tokens and textures, so VQA accuracy and grounding fail under object/background interventions; datasets lack counterfactual image–text pairs where image embeddings shift along non-causal dimensions while caption semantics are held fixed.
paraphrases.CV: In visual recognition, feature maps capture context-biased patterns at high spatial resolution, causing detectors that rely on anchor boxes to misclassify when object–context correlations are intervened (e.g., background swap), because segmentation masks and labels do not decouple object appearance from surrounding cues.
paraphrases.RL: In reinforcement learning, a policy can exploit proxy observations in the trajectory that correlate with the reward signal, so returns degrade under environment interventions that break these shortcuts; available rollouts provide insufficient coverage to identify causal state variables, harming sample efficiency.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, query–document scoring trained on click logs absorbs presentation and site priors in the embedding index, so recall@k and reranking quality collapse under interventions such as query reformulation or snippet debiasing; counterfactual permutations of the candidate pool are absent in logs.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In ASR, models rely on spectrogram channel characteristics and speaker-specific cues tied to transcripts, so WER rises when acoustic frames are intervened via codec, microphone, or accent shifts; parallel alignments that isolate phoneme content from nuisance factors are largely unavailable.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In manipulation, the sensorimotor loop exploits workspace-specific visual and layout shortcuts, so a manipulation policy fails under sim-to-real shifts or object appearance interventions that leave contact dynamics unchanged; experience buffers lack counterfactual variability needed for robust contact reasoning.

query_text:
Deep models are overconfident even when wrong, making
downstream decisions unreliable; how can we calibrate
predicted probabilities to reflect actual likelihood,
especially under distribution shift?
paraphrase_essence: Predicted confidences are systematically miscalibrated—typically overconfident relative to true error rates—and this mismatch worsens under distribution shift, undermining downstream decision reliability.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: In large language models, token-level probabilities emitted at each decoding step are overconfident relative to empirical error rates, so perplexity and confidence estimates fail to reflect true uncertainty across varying context length or out-of-distribution in-context examples, degrading reliability of generation-time decisions.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision–language models, query-conditioned attention over visual tokens yields answer and caption confidences that are miscalibrated with respect to actual VQA accuracy, and the gap widens when image embeddings or cross-modal alignment shift out of distribution, compromising trust in predicted probabilities.
paraphrases.CV: In dense visual perception, class and objectness scores derived from the feature map are overconfident: probabilities on anchor boxes and segmentation masks do not track true correctness, and miscalibration worsens with shifts in spatial resolution or receptive field statistics, reducing decision robustness.
paraphrases.RL: In reinforcement learning, policy action probabilities and value function estimates are miscalibrated relative to realized returns along trajectories, with overconfidence increasing under distribution shift between training rollouts and deployment, undermining safe exploration and control reliability.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In information retrieval, query-document scores from an embedding index or cross-encoder are overconfident and poorly calibrated to true relevance, so predicted probabilities diverge from observed click/relevance frequencies across the candidate pool, degrading recall@k under domain shift.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In automatic speech recognition, posterior probabilities over phonemes computed from acoustic frames and spectrograms are overconfident: alignment confidences fail to match observed error rates, and miscalibration increases under acoustic domain shift, inflating WER and undermining decision reliability.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In robotic manipulation, success probabilities assigned by a manipulation policy and its dynamics model are miscalibrated—overconfident about motion plans whose outcomes hinge on brittle contact reasoning—with the gap worsening under sim-to-real shift and compromising safety-critical decisions.

query_text:
Aligned text-image pairs are scarce in some domains while
unimodal data is abundant; how can we learn cross-modal
representations via contrastive objectives that exploit the
abundant unimodal data?
paraphrase_essence: Joint representation learning across modalities is bottlenecked by scarce aligned pairs, leaving abundant unimodal data unused for cross-modal alignment.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: Grounding text tokens to non-text context is constrained when paired sequences are scarce but most in-context examples are unimodal; attention heads receive little cross-token supervision, so perplexity gains from grounding do not materialize at practical context lengths.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Cross-modal alignment degrades when captioned image pairs are scarce while visual tokens and standalone text corpora are abundant; query-conditioned attention cannot reliably bind text spans to image embeddings, suppressing VQA accuracy and cross-modal retrieval quality.
paraphrases.CV: Dense visual understanding is limited when segmentation masks and bounding boxes are scarce despite abundant raw images; high–spatial-resolution feature maps lack object-level supervision, making anchor box assignment and fine-grained delineation unreliable.
paraphrases.RL: Policy learning is sample-inefficient when trajectories with calibrated reward signals are scarce but unlabeled rollouts are abundant; value function estimation and credit assignment degrade, impeding effective exploration.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Relevance estimation is brittle when labeled query-document pairs are scarce while the embedding index and corpus are large; cross-encoder reranking depends on query-document scores that cannot be robustly learned, lowering recall@k from sizable candidate pools.
paraphrases.SPEECH: ASR training falters when transcribed audio-text pairs are scarce while unlabeled spectrograms and text corpora are abundant; phoneme-level alignment over acoustic frames is underconstrained, yielding elevated WER and unstable decoding.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Language-conditioned control is brittle when instruction–action episodes are scarce while sensorimotor logs lack accompanying text; a manipulation policy cannot reliably bind linguistic goals to visual observations within the sensorimotor loop, degrading motion plan selection and contact reasoning.

seeds.lens: Grounding text tokens to non-text context is constrained when paired sequences are scarce but most in-context examples are unimodal; attention heads receive little cross-token supervision, so perplexity gains from grounding do not materialize at practical context lengths.

relevance.reasoning: The query asks about contrastive learning objectives that exploit abundant unimodal data to learn cross-modal representations when aligned pairs are scarce. query_text:
Masked-prediction pretraining transformed language and vision;
how can we adapt the same principle to other data types like
graphs, time series, or sensor streams, and what objective
design choices matter?
paraphrase_essence: Masking-based self-supervision fails to transfer across modalities when the corruption scheme and prediction target are misaligned with the modality’s structural dependencies, yielding weak learning signals and degraded downstream performance.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: In language modeling, perplexity degrades when token masking is misaligned with context length, causing attention heads to learn shortcut correlations and weakening the quality of in-context examples.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: In vision-language pretraining, cross-modal alignment deteriorates when masking of visual tokens and text spans ignores the granularity of image embeddings, leading query-conditioned attention to underfit and reducing VQA accuracy.
paraphrases.CV: In masked image modeling, reconstruction of patch tokens is unreliable when corruption disregards spatial resolution and receptive field, producing texture-biased feature maps and weaker segmentation masks.
paraphrases.RL: In reinforcement learning, representation objectives over masked segments of a trajectory fail when they sever temporal dependencies required by the policy and value function, reducing sample efficiency under sparse reward signals.
paraphrases.IR_REC: In retrieval, masking parts of queries or documents misaligns with query-document scoring, yielding an embedding index with poor recall@k and necessitating heavy cross-encoder reranking of the candidate pool.
paraphrases.SPEECH: In speech representation learning, masking acoustic frames or spectrogram regions disrupts phoneme alignment, emphasizing low-level reconstruction and increasing WER on downstream tasks.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: In robot learning from sensor streams, masking observations within the sensorimotor loop violates causal structure needed by the dynamics model, degrading contact reasoning and the resulting manipulation policy.

query_text:
Pretraining at scale is expensive; can we replace some real
data with synthetic curricula that exercise specific
capabilities, and how do we ensure synthetic data improves
rather than degrades downstream performance?
paraphrase_essence: Large-scale pretraining is constrained by the cost and availability of high-quality data; introducing lower-fidelity or distribution-shifted samples to reduce cost can distort learning signals and unpredictably degrade downstream generalization.

paraphrases.LLM_NLP: LLM pretraining is costly because long context length corpora drive billions of token updates; mixing in synthetic sequences with mismatched discourse statistics skews attention head activations and raises perplexity on downstream evaluations.
paraphrases.MULTIMODAL: Image–text pretraining is expensive due to the volume of visual tokens and image embeddings required, and replacing real captions with synthetic descriptions can disrupt cross-modal alignment and reduce VQA accuracy under distribution shift.
paraphrases.CV: Pretraining vision encoders on high spatial resolution images is costly, and injecting synthetic renderings can alter feature map statistics across patch tokens and receptive fields, degrading transfer to detection and segmentation mask quality.
paraphrases.RL: Collecting diverse trajectories for policy pretraining is expensive, and substituting scripted rollouts or proxy reward signals can bias behavior and hurt sample efficiency and downstream control performance.
paraphrases.IR_REC: Training retrieval models at scale is costly because supervised query–document signals are limited; adding synthetic queries into the candidate pool can distort query-document score distributions and lower recall@k after reranking.
paraphrases.SPEECH: Large-vocabulary ASR pretraining is expensive due to scarce labeled acoustic frames, and replacing real utterances with TTS-like spectrograms or phoneme-level templating can break alignment and increase WER on downstream benchmarks.
paraphrases.ROBOTICS: Data collection for manipulation policy learning is costly because closing the sensorimotor loop on hardware is slow, and substituting simulated demonstrations from a simplified dynamics model can exacerbate sim-to-real gaps and impair contact reasoning in real deployments.
